tldr: Matt — 66 live ads, but roughly 70% are translation clones of about 10 underlying creatives. The Leo Gregory tee block and the 'Out with the old. In with the new.' new-drop set are stacking frequency fast and will start dragging CPMs inside 7-12 days.

benchmark.context: Streetwear brands at your scale typically refresh hero creative every 3 weeks. You're effectively running ~10 unique concepts dressed up as 66 ads through EN/FR/DE/IT splits, which is why your fatigue clock is twice as fast as the category median.

ads.headline: default_collection_headline
ads.body: Weekend Offender: Fresh tones and statement graphics designed with sharp focus on functionality and detail.
drivers: Headline reads 'default_collection_headline' — that's a placeholder string that shipped live, so the ad is running with no real headline at all and the body is carrying the whole click.
Body copy is duplicated verbatim across five of your Leo Gregory variants in EN/DE/IT/FR — Meta's algo is treating these as one creative for frequency.
Generic catalog feed landing with no product hero in the hook — CTR will keep sliding as the audience saturates.

ads.headline: default_collection_headline
ads.body: Weekend Offender: Fresh tones and statement graphics designed with sharp focus on functionality and detail.
drivers: Another 'default_collection_headline' placeholder live in-feed — second ad shipping with the literal template variable instead of a headline.
Same body string as the English Leo Gregory block, pointing at the same navy tee SKU — it's cannibalizing its own siblings.
No hook differentiation: identical copy + identical destination + identical CTA across the cluster.

ads.headline: default_collection_headline
ads.body: Weekend Offender: Frische Töne und markante Grafiken, entwickelt mit klarem Fokus auf Funktionalität und Details.
drivers: Third placeholder-headline ad live, this one on the DE feed — the templating bug is affecting multiple markets.
DE body is a 1:1 translation of the EN Leo Gregory line, no localization beyond Google-Translate-grade phrasing.
Same Leo Gregory navy SKU pile-up — DACH audience is seeing the same shirt three different ways.

ads.headline: default_collection_headline
ads.body: Weekend Offender: Toni freschi e grafiche decise, progettati con una forte attenzione alla funzionalità e ai dettagli.
drivers: Italian-market version with the same 'default_collection_headline' placeholder — fourth ad with a broken headline field.
Body is the IT translation of the same Leo Gregory script — zero creative variance vs. the EN/DE/FR siblings.
Routes to the white tee variant while three other live ads route to the navy — splitting the same buyer across SKUs.

ads.headline: Weekend Offender's Best
ads.body: The Overnighter. A fan favourite for a reason. Crafted from high-density weave 100% polyester with a compact, cabin-ready design that fits aeroplane hand luggage. Built tough. Travels light. Explore our bestsellers.
drivers: Exact same Overnighter body copy is running on at least three near-identical English ads — same headline, same destination, same UTM term.
'Built tough. Travels light.' has been in-market long enough that warm audiences will have seen this exact bag photo dozens of times.
No price anchor, no social proof, no scarcity — pure product description, which is the first format to fatigue on retargeting.

ads_compact.headline: Weekend Offender
ads_compact.body: The Technician Jacket with mesh-lining, water-repellent coating and built-in face mask for protection against the elements.

ads_compact.headline: Weekend Offender
ads_compact.body: Music. Rave. Terrace. Film. Designed in the UK. Premium graphic tees at Weekend Offender.

ads_compact.headline: New spring '26 collection.
ads_compact.body: New silhouettes. Fresh palette with softer tones and considered contrasts.

hero_concept.format: Static image (1:1) — first in a 3-card carousel: hero shot, fabric/detail macro, on-body terrace shot
hero_concept.hook: Built for the away end. Cut for the city.
hero_concept.headline: Grohl Over-Top. Now in Cyclone.
hero_concept.primary_text:
The Grohl Over-Top in Cyclone Grey Camo isn't a shell jacket pretending to be streetwear. It's a terrace piece — designed in the UK, weighted to layer over a hood, cut to move when the crowd does.

Music. Rave. Terrace. Film. The same four reference points every Weekend Offender drop is built around — this one just happens to be the one you'll actually wear from March through October.

New season. New camo. Limited run — when it's gone, it's gone.
hero_concept.visual_direction: Single hero shot of the Grohl Over-Top Cyclone Grey Camo on a clean concrete-grey backdrop, slight overhead angle, garment laid with intentional folds showing the camo pattern and over-top silhouette.
hero_concept.fills_gap: Every current ad either runs the brand name as the headline or the raw Shopify product title — there's no creative that names a specific hero piece with a story-led terrace hook. This concept gives one product a clean spotlight instead of yet another 'Weekend Offender / new drop' generic.
hero_concept.image_prompt: A photorealistic editorial product photograph featuring the product from the reference image, styled in a minimalist concrete and steel studio set with brushed grey backdrop and a single weathered metal bench, with directional cool daylight from camera-left creating soft shadows and crisp fabric texture. Moody UK streetwear aesthetic, muted palette of slate, charcoal and pale concrete, cinematic editorial framing reminiscent of British terrace and rave-culture lookbooks. Square 1:1 composition with breathing room top and bottom for ad text overlay. Maintain the exact appearance, color, and details of the product from the reference image. Premium commerce photography quality. NEGATIVE: no text, no logos, no watermarks, no UI elements, no Facebook interface, no buttons, no faces, no collage, no borders, no duplicate products.

next_step.urgency: Four ads hit fatigue inside 8 days — including three with a broken 'default_collection_headline' placeholder live in-feed.
next_step.headline: 30 minutes — let's fix the placeholder bug and ship the SS26 hero
next_step.body: I want to walk you through the four ads bleeding budget right now and the Grohl Over-Top concept that should replace them. Half the call is the fix list, half is what we'd queue for the next two weeks.

method_note: Scored from public Meta Ad Library creatives only — no spend or CTR data, so timing is directional based on creative-signal patterns across all 66 live ads.
